title: Parameterized Access, Authorization, Privacy, and Audit · Relational Lens: Injection Boundaries
summary: A web service accepts user filters, exposes support data by role, and must resist injection and excessive collection. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql injection boundaries increment in a least-privilege parameterized data-access contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify code-data boundary failures in dynamic SQL and demonstrate why escaping alone is incomplete.
Benchmark representative warmed and cold cases, verify unchanged results, and account for write amplification.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A viewer submits severity text "high' OR 1=1 --" and sort direction "desc" while requesting member details. Bind values, allow-list structure, minimize returned fields, apply least privilege, and redact audit evidence. A defensible solution applies Injection Boundaries, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a least-privilege parameterized data-access contract.

content.code:
-- Application contract: prepare once and bind :severity as data.
SELECT ticket_id, title, status
FROM tickets
WHERE severity = 'high'
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- Structural choices such as sort direction require an application allow-list.
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Injection Boundaries evidence · Theory Injection Boundaries
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-injection-boundaries-o37xdh" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; parameterization, authorization, minimization, or audit evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Injection Boundaries evidence · Theory Injection Boundaries.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Injection Boundaries into observable performance evidence. For Build Injection Boundaries evidence · Theory Injection Boundaries, keep Injection Boundaries connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-injection-boundaries-o37xdh
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id, title, status
FROM tickets
WHERE status = :status AND team_id = :authorized_team_id
ORDER BY ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
